---
title: "Analysis of fragmented river systems"
author: "Damiano Baldan"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: rmdformats::downcute
knit: (function(input_file, encoding) {
  out_dir <- 'docs';
  rmarkdown::render(input_file,
 encoding=encoding,
 output_file=file.path(dirname(input_file), out_dir, 'index.html'))})
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Content

This Github repository contains the materials for the course `Analysis of fragmented river systems`. The Markdown scripts of the lectures are available in the root of the repository. The data used in the lectures are available in the `data` folder.

# List of lectures

-   [lecture 1](https://damianobaldan.github.io/riverconn_course/1_data_processing.html)

-   [lecture 2](https://damianobaldan.github.io/riverconn_course/2_river_networks.html)

-   [lecture 3](https://damianobaldan.github.io/riverconn_course/2_river_networks.html)

-   [lecture 4](https://damianobaldan.github.io/riverconn_course/2_river_networks.html)


# Important papers

-   The `riverconn` paper: [Baldan et al., 2022](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.envsoft.2022.105470)

-   An example of riverconn application for fish metacommunities: [Baldan et al., 2023](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.scitotenv.2023.166703)

-   A nice review on fragmentation indices: [Jumani et al., 2022](https://doi.org/10.1088/1748-9326/abcb37)

-   The original `DCI` paper: [Cote et al., 2009](https://doi.org/10.1007/s10980-008-9283-y)


# some `riverconn` applications

-   [Kowal et al., 2024](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ecolind.2024.112130)
-   [Felin et al., 2025](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.scitotenv.2024.178204)
-   [Yofukuji et al., 2026](https://doi.org/10.1111/gcb.70685)
-   [Sun et al., 2025](https://doi.org/10.1038/s43247-025-02467-y)
-   [Médice-Fernandes et al., 2026](https://doi.org/10.1002/aqc.70342)
